description: Zero-config, modular monitoring SDK for Node.js applications.

core_concepts.zero_config_magic: SDK automatically patches popular frameworks (Express, Axios, Fetch) so developers don't have to write manual try/catch or middleware.
core_concepts.breadcrumb_timeline: Captures a circular buffer of last 50 events (requests, db calls, custom logs) to provide context for every incident.

severity_levels.SEV1: Critical crash (e.g., Uncaught Exception) - process exit.
severity_levels.SEV2: Significant failure (e.g., Unhandled Rejection, 5xx Express errors).

core_concepts.heartbeat_system: Sends a 'pulse' every 5 seconds (configurable via heartbeatIntervalMs) containing the latest terminal logs (stdout/stderr) and system health metrics (uptime, memory, CPU) for real-time monitoring.

bootstrap_flow: Config module validates inputs and merges with defaults.
Winston Logger initialized for structured logging.
HttpTransport setup with Axios, Retries, and Circuit Breaker.
MemoryMonitor starts a timer to check heap usage every 30 seconds.
Integrations (Express, Axios, Fetch) are monkey-patched to intercept activity.

features: Auto-injects Error Middleware at the end of the stack.
Auto-injects Morgan HTTP Logger at the start of the stack.
Wraps async route handlers to catch hidden promise rejections.
Captures outgoing request metadata as breadcrumbs.
Captures 5xx and network errors as incidents.
Tracks response duration and failure status.

reliability_features.exponential_backoff: Retries failed incident delivery with increasing delays (1s, 2s, 4s).
reliability_features.circuit_breaker: Temporarily stops sending incidents if the platform is unreachable for 5+ consecutive attempts to prevent memory bloat.
reliability_features.unref_timers: Uses .unref() on internal timers so the SDK doesn't prevent the Node process from exiting naturally.
